title: Two-Step Cage
setup: Kings face off on the **d-file**, your queen lurks on **d1** ready to climb up with check.
hint: Step the queen up with check, drive the king toward the corner, then sweep the back rank for mate.
explanation:
## The Idea

A classic **K+Q vs K mate-in-2**. Move 1 forces the king sideways, move 2 ends it on the back rank.

**1. {{Qd5+}}** — the queen jumps up the file with check. Black's king is on **d8** and must move; the only square not covered by your king on **d6** or attacked by the queen is **c8**.

**1...{{Kc8}}** — forced. {{Kxd5}} is illegal (your king defends d5), {{Ke8}} is also legal but loses the same way; we follow the textbook line.

**2. {{Qa8#}}** — the queen sweeps to the corner of the back rank, delivering mate.

Why is the king stuck after move 2?

- {{b8}} — attacked by the queen along the 8th rank
- {{c7}}, {{d7}} — both controlled by your king on **d6**
- The queen is far from the king, so {{Kxa8}} is impossible
- Nothing can interpose on the 8th rank

> **Takeaway:** In K+Q endings, the queen does the cutting and your king does the *covering*. Always pair queen checks with king support, and march toward a back-rank or edge mate.
